# External Comment Rules and Examples

## Rules

External comments should be firm but commercial. Do not mention internal ratings, approval thresholds, or “red flag.” Use language such as:
- “We suggest aligning this clause with the agreed SOW structure.”
- “We can accept this concept if limited to the relevant SOW and subject to the liability cap.”
- “This obligation should apply only to matters within Vendor’s reasonable control.”

## Examples

| Issue | External comment |
| --- | --- |
| Unlimited liability | We suggest aligning the liability cap with the commercial scope and value of the relevant SOW. The proposed cap should apply subject to the agreed exceptions. |
| IP before payment | We can transfer ownership in the applicable deliverables once the related fees are paid. This keeps IP ownership aligned with the payment structure. |
| Client-caused delay | Because delivery depends on timely access, instructions, and approvals, we suggest adding a time extension for delays caused by late client inputs. |
| Background IP | We are comfortable assigning client-specific deliverables, but our pre-existing tools, methods, libraries, templates, and general know-how must remain excluded. |
| GenAI | We can commit to responsible and secure use of AI tools and to not use client confidential information for model training outside the agreement. |
| Security audit | We can provide reasonable security documentation and discuss targeted audit rights, subject to confidentiality, notice, scope, and non-disruption safeguards. |
